ogTitle: Dutton Park vs Dykehead: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dutton Park and Dykehead.
heading: Dutton Park vs Dykehead — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dutton Park, Queensland and Dykehead, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dykehead has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Dutton Park (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dutton Park or Dykehead safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dutton Park has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Dykehead scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dutton Park scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Dykehead scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dutton Park or Dykehead?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dykehead has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
